questions.question: What is the main purpose of HTML in a web page?
options: To control visual design and layout
To store data in the browser
questions.explanation: HTML is used to structure content and describe what each part of the page is, such as headings, paragraphs, links, images, and forms.

option_explanations.To control visual design and layout: This is mainly the job of CSS, which controls appearance and layout.
option_explanations.To add interactivity and logic: This is mainly the job of JavaScript, which controls behaviour and events.
option_explanations.To structure and describe content: This is correct because HTML gives the page its structure and meaning.
option_explanations.To store data in the browser: This is not HTML’s purpose. Browser storage is usually handled with JavaScript features like localStorage.

questions.question: Which HTML element is used for the largest page heading?
questions.explanation: The <h1> element is used for the main heading of a page and is the highest heading level in HTML.

option_explanations.<heading>: This is not a valid standard HTML element.
option_explanations.<h1>: This is correct because <h1> represents the main heading.
option_explanations.<title>: The <title> element is placed in the head section and sets the browser tab title, not the visible page heading.
option_explanations.<header>: The <header> element is a semantic container, not a heading level itself.

questions.question: Which attribute is used in an anchor tag to define the destination link?
questions.explanation: The href attribute stores the destination URL or file path for an anchor tag.

option_explanations.src: src is commonly used for resources such as images or scripts, not normal links.
option_explanations.href: This is correct because href tells the browser where the link should go.
option_explanations.alt: alt is used with images to provide alternative text.
option_explanations.class: class is used for styling or selecting elements, not for link destinations.

questions.question: Why is the alt attribute important in an image element?
options: It changes the border color of the image
It adds animation to the image
It provides alternative text and improves accessibility
questions.answer: It provides alternative text and improves accessibility
questions.explanation: The alt attribute gives a text description of the image, which is useful if the image cannot load or for assistive technologies like screen readers.

option_explanations.It changes the border color of the image: Border color is controlled with CSS, not the alt attribute.
option_explanations.It adds animation to the image: Animation is not controlled by alt text.
option_explanations.It provides alternative text and improve: This is correct because alt text helps users understand the image content.
option_explanations.It makes the image larger: Image size is controlled by CSS or width/height attributes, not alt.

questions.question: Which HTML element is most appropriate for a list of navigation links?
questions.explanation: The <nav> element is specifically intended for navigation links and menus.

option_explanations.<footer>: A footer is used for closing page content, not specifically for navigation.
option_explanations.<nav>: This is correct because <nav> is the semantic element for navigation links.
option_explanations.<article>: An article is used for standalone content such as a blog post or news item.
option_explanations.<aside>: An aside is used for related but secondary content, not main navigation.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of CSS?
questions.explanation: CSS is used to control the visual presentation of a page, including colors, fonts, spacing, layout, and responsiveness.

option_explanations.To structure content: Structure is mainly handled by HTML.
option_explanations.To style and format content: This is correct because CSS controls presentation and visual design.
option_explanations.To add event handling: Event handling is usually done with JavaScript.
option_explanations.To load JSON files: Loading files is handled with JavaScript, not CSS.

questions.question: In the rule 'p { color: blue; }', what is 'p'?
questions.explanation: In CSS, the selector chooses which elements the rule applies to. In this case, all paragraph elements are targeted.

option_explanations.A property: The property here is color, not p.
option_explanations.A selector: This is correct because p selects all paragraph elements.
option_explanations.A value: The value here is blue, not p.
option_explanations.A class: A class selector would normally start with a dot, such as .card.

questions.question: Which CSS property changes the inside spacing of an element?
questions.explanation: Padding controls the space inside an element, between the content and the border.

option_explanations.margin: Margin controls outside spacing, not inside spacing.
option_explanations.padding: This is correct because padding changes the inner spacing.
option_explanations.border: Border controls the visible line around an element, not spacing itself.
option_explanations.display: Display changes layout behaviour, not inside spacing.

questions.question: Which approach is usually best for styling multiple pages consistently?
questions.explanation: An external stylesheet allows styles to be reused and managed more easily across multiple pages.

option_explanations.Inline CSS on every element: This becomes repetitive and difficult to maintain.
option_explanations.A separate external stylesheet: This is correct because it keeps styles reusable and organised.
option_explanations.Writing all styles in JavaScript: This is possible in some cases, but it is not the normal or best approach for standard page styling.
option_explanations.Using only browser defaults: This would not give a customised and polished design.

questions.question: What does responsive design aim to do?
options: Make pages adapt to different screen sizes
Remove all colors from the page
questions.answer: Make pages adapt to different screen sizes
questions.explanation: Responsive design helps webpages look and work well on desktops, tablets, and phones.

option_explanations.Make pages load slower: This is not the goal of responsive design.
option_explanations.Make pages adapt to different screen siz: This is correct because responsive design improves usability on different devices.
option_explanations.Remove all colors from the page: Responsive design is about layout and usability, not removing colour.
option_explanations.Replace HTML with CSS: CSS does not replace HTML; they work together.

questions.question: What is JavaScript mainly used for in front-end web development?
questions.explanation: JavaScript is mainly used to make the page interactive by responding to user actions and updating content dynamically.

option_explanations.Defining semantic structure only: That is mainly the role of HTML.
option_explanations.Styling page colors only: That is mainly the role of CSS.
option_explanations.Adding interactivity and dynamic behavio: This is correct because JavaScript handles events, logic, and dynamic updates.
option_explanations.Replacing HTML headings: JavaScript can change headings, but that is not its main purpose.

questions.question: What does the DOM represent?
options: The browser’s model of the webpage structure
questions.answer: The browser’s model of the webpage structure
questions.explanation: The DOM is the browser’s internal representation of the HTML document, which JavaScript can read and change.

option_explanations.A database manager: The DOM is not related to databases.
option_explanations.The browser’s model of the webpage struc: This is correct because the DOM represents the page as objects in memory.
option_explanations.A CSS animation library: The DOM is not a library.
option_explanations.A type of image format: The DOM is not an image format.

questions.question: Which function is commonly used to display debugging information in the browser console?
questions.explanation: console.log() is widely used to print values and messages into the browser console for debugging.

option_explanations.print(): This is not the standard browser debugging function for console output.
option_explanations.write(): This is not the common console debugging method.
option_explanations.console.log(): This is correct because it outputs debugging information to the console.
option_explanations.debug.text(): This is not a standard JavaScript debugging function.

questions.question: What is an event listener used for?
options: To change CSS files into HTML
To wait for a user action and run code
To store files in local storage
questions.answer: To wait for a user action and run code
questions.explanation: An event listener listens for events such as clicks or input changes and then runs a function in response.

option_explanations.To change CSS files into HTML: Event listeners do not convert file types.
option_explanations.To wait for a user action and run code: This is correct because event listeners react to events.
option_explanations.To store files in local storage: Local storage is separate from event listeners.
option_explanations.To make images responsive: Responsive images are handled with HTML/CSS, not event listeners.

questions.question: Why is loading quiz questions from a JSON file useful in this project?
options: Because it allows the questions to be managed separately from the HTML
Because it removes the need for answers
questions.answer: Because it allows the questions to be managed separately from the HTML
questions.explanation: Using a JSON file keeps question data separate from the page structure and allows the quiz to be rendered dynamically with JavaScript.

option_explanations.Because it avoids using JavaScript: Loading JSON dynamically still uses JavaScript.
option_explanations.Because it allows the questions to be ma: This is correct because it keeps the data modular and easier to maintain.
option_explanations.Because it removes the need for answers: The quiz still needs correct answers to calculate results.
option_explanations.Because it makes CSS optional: CSS is unrelated to whether JSON is used.
